941358249.name: ReadWebpage

941358249.content:
Bos, saya sudah periksa `skill.json`-nya. Ternyata ini adalah **CLI Tool** (`npx ui-ux-pro-max-cli init`), bukan *library* kode biasa yang bisa saya *copy-paste* filenya secara manual ke direktori kita.

Karena ini berbasis CLI, cara kerjanya adalah dengan menginisialisasi *design system* di proyek kita.

**Saran saya:**
Daripada saya memaksakan mengunduh kode *source* yang tidak didesain untuk di-*import* langsung, saya bisa bertindak sebagai **"UI/UX Pro Max Engine"** itu sendiri. Karena `SKILL.md` sudah saya pasang, saya sudah memiliki akses ke instruksi dan *guidelines* dari *skill* tersebut.

Bos cukup panggil `@ui-ux-pro-max` kapan saja saat butuh saran desain, dan saya akan memberikan jawaban yang *sesuai dengan aturan dan logika* dari *skill* tersebut (84 gaya UI, 192 palet warna, dsb).

Apakah Bos ingin kita mulai mencoba fitur desainnya sekarang? Misalnya, mau buat sistem desain untuk halaman apa?
oke jarvish balik lagi ke /home/johnwick/Project/belajar-nextjs di sana saya sudah membuat skills di bisa cek satu satu tetapi dalam nya sama ada ui-ux-pro-max

941358249.name: SystemCommand

941358249.content:
Wah, lengkap sekali isinya, Bos! Ternyata di dalam `/home/johnwick/Project/belajar-nextjs/.gemini/skills` ini tersimpan banyak *skill* yang terorganisir dengan rapi, mulai dari `brand`, `design`, sampai `ui-ux-pro-max`.

Tiap folder memiliki struktur yang sangat profesional:
- **`SKILL.md`**: Definisinya.
- **`references/`**: Dokumentasi pendukung/panduan.
- **`scripts/`** (di beberapa folder): *Tools* otomasi untuk menjalankan *skill* tersebut.

Ini adalah *setup* yang sangat canggih untuk pengembangan UI/UX dan branding. Bos ingin saya melakukan apa dengan *skill-skill* ini? Apakah mau saya integrasikan ke dalam alur kerja kita sekarang, atau ada file tertentu yang ingin Bos pelajari/edit?
ah sekrang kamu implementasi buat page landing menggunakan @ui-ux-pro-max dan @desain

--- /home/johnwick/Project/belajar-nextjs/.gemini/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/SKILL.md (Lines 1-383 of 383) ---
1: ---
2: name: ui-ux-pro-max
3: description: UI/UX design intelligence with searchable database
4: ---
5: # ui-ux-pro-max
6: 
7: Comprehensive design guide for web, mobile, and desktop applications. Contains 67 styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 22 technology stacks. Searchable database with priority-based recommendations.
8: 
9: # Prerequisites
10: 
11: The bundled scripts require Python 3 (standard library only — no third-party packages, no network access). Check if it is available:
12: 
13: ```bash
14: python3 --version || python --version
15: ```
16: 
17: If Python is not installed, **do not install it yourself**. Stop and ask the user to install Python 3 using their preferred method (e.g. from [python.org](https://www.python.org/downloads/) or their OS package manager), then continue once it is available. Never run package-manager or system-modifying commands (`sudo`, `brew`, `apt`, `winget`, etc.) on the user's machine for this skill.
18: 
19: If the user prefers not to install Python, skip the CLI searches and rely on the Quick Reference sections above.
20: 
21: > **Note:** On Windows, use `python` instead of `python3` to run scripts (e.g., `python scripts/search.py` instead of `python3 scripts/search.py`).
22: 
23: ---
24: 
25: ## How to Use This Skill
26: 
27: Use this skill when the user requests any of the following:
28: 
29: | Scenario | Trigger Examples | Start From |
30: |----------|-----------------|------------|
31: | **New project / page** | "做一个 landing page"、"Build a dashboard" | Step 1 → Step 2 (design system) |
32: | **New component** | "Create a pricing card"、"Add a modal" | Step 3 (domain search: style, ux) |
33: | **Choose style / color / font** | "What style fits a fintech app?"、"推荐配色" | Step 2 (design system) |
34: | **Review existing UI** | "Review this page for UX issues"、"检查无障碍" | Quick Reference checklist above |
35: | **Fix a UI bug** | "Button hover is broken"、"Layout shifts on load" | Quick Reference → relevant section |
36: | **Improve / optimize** | "Make this faster"、"Improve mobile experience" | Step 3 (domain search: ux, react) |
37: | **Implement dark mode** | "Add dark mode support" | Step 3 (domain: style "dark mode") |
38: | **Add charts / data viz** | "Add an analytics dashboard chart" | Step 3 (domain: chart) |
39: | **Stack best practices** | "React performance tips"、"SwiftUI navigation" | Step 4 (stack search) |
40: 
41: Follow this workflow:
42: 
43: ### Step 1: Analyze User Requirements
44: 
45: Extract key information from user request:
46: - **Product type**: Entertainment (social, video, music, gaming), Tool (scanner, editor, converter), Productivity (task manager, notes, calendar), or hybrid
47: - **Target audience**: C-end consumer users; consider age group, usage context (commute, leisure, work)
48: - **Style keywords**: playful, vibrant, minimal, dark mode, content-first, immersive, etc.
49: - **Stack**: React Native (this project's only tech stack)
50: 
51: ### Step 2: Generate Design System (REQUIRED)
52: 
53: **Always start with `--design-system`** to get comprehensive recommendations with reasoning:
54: 
55: ```bash
56: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<product_type> <industry> <keywords>" --design-system [-p "Project Name"]
57: ```
58: 
59: This command:
60: 1. Searches domains in parallel (product, style, color, landing, typography)
61: 2. Applies reasoning rules from `ui-reasoning.csv` to select best matches
62: 3. Returns complete design system: pattern, style, colors, typography, effects
63: 4. Includes anti-patterns to avoid
64: 
65: **Example:**
66: ```bash
67: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "beauty spa wellness service" --design-system -p "Serenity Spa"
68: ```
69: 
70: ### Step 2b: Persist Design System (Master + Overrides Pattern)
71: 
72: To save the design system for **hierarchical retrieval across sessions**, add `--persist`:
73: 
74: ```bash
75: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<query>" --design-system --persist -p "Project Name"
76: ```
77: 
78: This creates:
79: - `design-system/MASTER.md` — Global Source of Truth with all design rules
80: - `design-system/pages/` — Folder for page-specific overrides
81: 
82: **With page-specific override:**
83: ```bash
84: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<query>" --design-system --persist -p "Project Name" --page "dashboard"
85: ```
86: 
87: This also creates:
88: - `design-system/pages/dashboard.md` — Page-specific deviations from Master
89: 
90: **How hierarchical retrieval works:**
91: 1. When building a specific page (e.g., "Checkout"), first check `design-system/pages/checkout.md`
92: 2. If the page file exists, its rules **override** the Master file
93: 3. If not, use `design-system/MASTER.md` exclusively
94: 
95: **Context-aware retrieval prompt:**
96: ```
97: I am building the [Page Name] page. Please read design-system/MASTER.md.
98: Also check if design-system/pages/[page-name].md exists.
99: If the page file exists, prioritize its rules.
100: If not, use the Master rules exclusively.
101: Now, generate the code...
102: ```
103: 
104: ### Step 2c: Design Dials (optional)
105: 
106: Three optional 1-10 sliders that tune `--design-system` output without changing your query. Add any combination of them to the same command:
107: 
108: ```bash
109: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<query>" --design-system --variance <1-10> --motion <1-10> --density <1-10>
110: ```
111: 
112: | Dial | Low (1-3) | Mid (4-7) | High (8-10) |
113: |------|-----------|-----------|-------------|
114: | `--variance` | Centered / minimal (biases toward Minimalism-style categories) | Balanced / modern | Bold / asymmetric (biases toward Brutalism, Bento Grids) |
115: | `--motion` | Subtle micro-interactions | Standard scroll/stagger motion | Complex choreography (pin, Flip, SplitText) |
116: | `--density` | Spacious (24-96px spacing scale) | Standard (16-64px, current default) | Dense/dashboard (8-32px spacing scale) |
117: 
118: - `--motion` attaches a ready-to-use GSAP snippet (with framework notes, Do/Don't, and performance notes) pulled from `--domain gsap`, matched to the resolved tier (Subtle/Standard/Complex).
119: - `--density` overrides the `--space-*` CSS variable table in the ASCII/markdown/MASTER.md output — use it for dashboards (high) vs. marketing pages (low) without hand-editing tokens.
120: - Leaving a dial unset keeps that part of the output exactly as it was before (no behavior change).
121: 
122: **Example:**
123: ```bash
124: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "internal analytics dashboard" --design-system --variance 8 --motion 7 --density 8 -p "Ops Console"
125: ```
126: 
127: ### Step 3: Supplement with Detailed Searches (as needed)
128: 
129: After getting the design system, use domain searches to get additional details:
130: 
131: ```bash
132: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<keyword>" --domain <domain> [-n <max_results>]
133: ```
134: 
135: **When to use detailed searches:**
136: 
137: | Need | Domain | Example |
138: |------|--------|---------|
139: | Product type patterns | `product` | `--domain product "entertainment social"` |
140: | More style options | `style` | `--domain style "glassmorphism dark"` |
141: | Color palettes | `color` | `--domain color "entertainment vibrant"` |
142: | Font pairings | `typography` | `--domain typography "playful modern"` |
143: | Chart recommendations | `chart` | `--domain chart "real-time dashboard"` |
144: | UX best practices | `ux` | `--domain ux "animation accessibility"` |
145: | Landing structure | `landing` | `--domain landing "hero social-proof"` |
146: | React Native perf | `react` | `--domain react "rerender memo list"` |
147: | App interface a11y | `web` | `--domain web "accessibilityLabel touch safe-areas"` |
148: | AI prompt / CSS keywords | `prompt` | `--domain prompt "minimalism"` |
149: 
150: ### Step 4: Stack Guidelines
151: 
152: Get implementation-specific best practices for the user's stack:
153: 
154: ```bash
155: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<keyword>" --stack <stack>
156: ```
157: 
158: ---
159: 
160: ## Search Reference
161: 
162: ### Available Domains
163: 
164: | Domain | Use For | Example Keywords |
165: |--------|---------|------------------|
166: | `product` | Product type recommendations | SaaS, e-commerce, portfolio, healthcare, beauty, service |
167: | `style` | UI styles, colors, effects | glassmorphism, minimalism, dark mode, brutalism |
168: | `typography` | Font pairings, Google Fonts | elegant, playful, professional, modern |
169: | `color` | Color palettes by product type | saas, ecommerce, healthcare, beauty, fintech, service |
170: | `landing` | Page structure, CTA strategies | hero, hero-centric, testimonial, pricing, social-proof |
171: | `chart` | Chart types, library recommendations | trend, comparison, timeline, funnel, pie |
172: | `ux` | Best practices, anti-patterns | animation, accessibility, z-index, loading |
173: | `gsap` | GSAP animation skeletons by intensity tier | scroll reveal, stagger, magnetic cursor, page transition |
174: | `react` | React/Next.js performance | waterfall, bundle, suspense, memo, rerender, cache |
175: | `web` | App interface guidelines (iOS/Android/React Native) | accessibilityLabel, touch targets, safe areas, Dynamic Type |
176: | `prompt` | AI prompts, CSS keywords | (style name) |
177: 
178: ### Available Stacks
179: 
180: | Stack | Focus |
181: |-------|-------|
182: | `react-native` | Components, Navigation, Lists |
183: | `javafx` | Enterprise desktop apps, AtlantaFX themes, FXML, CSS, Controls, Binding, Threading, Packaging |
184: 
185: **JavaFX enterprise examples:**
186: 
187: ```bash
188: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "atlantafx primer enterprise theme" --stack javafx
189: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "enterprise tableview density permission" --stack javafx
190: ```
191: 
192: ---
193: 
194: ## Example Workflow
195: 
196: **User request:** "Make an AI search homepage。"
197: 
198: ### Step 1: Analyze Requirements
199: - Product type: Tool (AI search engine)
200: - Target audience: C-end users looking for fast, intelligent search
201: - Style keywords: modern, minimal, content-first, dark mode
202: - Stack: React Native
203: 
204: ### Step 2: Generate Design System (REQUIRED)
205: 
206: ```bash
207: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "AI search tool modern minimal" --design-system -p "AI Search"
208: ```
209: 
210: **Output:** Complete design system with pattern, style, colors, typography, effects, and anti-patterns.
211: 
212: ### Step 3: Supplement with Detailed Searches (as needed)
213: 
214: ```bash
215: # Get style options for a modern tool product
216: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "minimalism dark mode" --domain style
217: 
218: # Get UX best practices for search interaction and loading
219: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "search loading animation" --domain ux
220: ```
221: 
222: ### Step 4: Stack Guidelines
223: 
224: ```bash
225: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "list performance navigation" --stack react-native
226: ```
227: 
228: **Then:** Synthesize design system + detailed searches and implement the design.
229: 
230: ---
231: 
232: ## Output Formats
233: 
234: The `--design-system` flag supports two output formats:
235: 
236: ```bash
237: # ASCII box (default) - best for terminal display
238: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "fintech crypto" --design-system
239: 
240: # Markdown - best for documentation
241: python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "fintech crypto" --design-system -f markdown
242: ```
243: 
244: ---
245: 
246: ## Tips for Better Results
247: 
248: ### Query Strategy
249: 
250: - Use **multi-dimensional keywords** — combine product + industry + tone + density: `"entertainment social vibrant content-dense"` not just `"app"`
251: - Try different keywords for the same need: `"playful neon"` → `"vibrant dark"` → `"content-first minimal"`
252: - Use `--design-system` first for full recommendations, then `--domain` to deep-dive any dimension you're unsure about
253: - Add `--stack <stack>` for implementation-specific guidance when the target stack is known
254: 
255: ### Common Sticking Points
256: 
257: | Problem | What to Do |
258: |---------|------------|
259: | Can't decide on style/color | Re-run `--design-system` with different keywords |
260: | Dark mode contrast issues | Quick Reference §6: `color-dark-mode` + `color-accessible-pairs` |
261: | Animations feel unnatural | Quick Reference §7: `spring-physics` + `easing` + `exit-faster-than-enter` |
262: | Form UX is poor | Quick Reference §8: `inline-validation` + `error-clarity` + `focus-management` |
263: | Navigation feels confusing | Quick Reference §9: `nav-hierarchy` + `bottom-nav-limit` + `back-behavior` |
264: | Layout breaks on small screens | Quick Reference §5: `mobile-first` + `breakpoint-consistency` |
265: | Performance / jank | Quick Reference §3: `virtualize-lists` + `main-thread-budget` + `debounce-throttle` |
266: 
267: ### Pre-Delivery Checklist
268: 
269: - Run `--domain ux "animation accessibility z-index loading"` as a UX validation pass before implementation
270: - Run through Quick Reference **§1–§3** (CRITICAL + HIGH) as a final review
271: - Test on 375px (small phone) and landscape orientation
272: - Verify behavior with **reduced-motion** enabled and **Dynamic Type** at largest size
273: - Check dark mode contrast independently (don't assume light mode values work)
274: - Confirm all touch targets ≥44pt and no content hidden behind safe areas
275: 
276: ---
277: 
278: ## Common Rules for Professional UI
279: 
280: These are frequently overlooked issues that make UI look unprofessional:
281: Scope notice: The rules below are for App UI (iOS/Android/React Native/Flutter), not desktop-web interaction patterns.
282: 
283: ### Icons & Visual Elements
284: 
285: - 默认图标库使用 **Phosphor (`@phosphor-icons/react`)**。`src/ui-ux-pro-max/data/icons.csv` 中列出的只是常用推荐图标，不是完整集合。
286: - 当推荐表中找不到合适的图标时：
287:   - **优先继续从 Phosphor 的完整图标集中选择任何语义更贴切的图标**；
288:   - 如果 Phosphor 也没有理想选项，可以使用 **Heroicons (`@heroicons/react`)** 作为备选，注意保持风格一致（线性/填充、笔画粗细、圆角风格）。
289: 
290: | Rule | Standard | Avoid | Why It Matters |
291: |------|----------|--------|----------------|
292: | **No Emoji as Structural Icons** | Use vector-based icons (e.g., Phosphor `@phosphor-icons/react`, Heroicons `@heroicons/react`, react-native-vector-icons, @expo/vector-icons). | Using emojis (🎨 🚀 ⚙️) for navigation, settings, or system controls. | Emojis are font-dependent, inconsistent across platforms, and cannot be controlled via design tokens. |
293: | **Vector-Only Assets** | Use SVG or platform vector icons that scale cleanly and support theming. | Raster PNG icons that blur or pixelate. | Ensures scalability, crisp rendering, and dark/light mode adaptability. |
294: | **Stable Interaction States** | Use color, opacity, or elevation transitions for press states without changing layout bounds. | Layout-shifting transforms that move surrounding content or trigger visual jitter. | Prevents unstable interactions and preserves smooth motion/perceived quality on mobile. |
295: | **Correct Brand Logos** | Use official brand assets and follow their usage guidelines (spacing, color, clear space). | Guessing logo paths, recoloring unofficially, or modifying proportions. | Prevents brand misuse and ensures legal/platform compliance. |
296: | **Consistent Icon Sizing** | Define icon sizes as design tokens (e.g., icon-sm, icon-md = 24pt, icon-lg). | Mixing arbitrary values like 20pt / 24pt / 28pt randomly. | Maintains rhythm and visual hierarchy across the interface. |
297: | **Stroke Consistency** | Use a consistent stroke width within the same visual layer (e.g., 1.5px or 2px). | Mixing thick and thin stroke styles arbitrarily. | Inconsistent strokes reduce perceived polish and cohesion. |
298: | **Filled vs Outline Discipline** | Use one icon style per hierarchy level. | Mixing filled and outline icons at the same hierarchy level. | Maintains semantic clarity and stylistic coherence. |
299: | **Touch Target Minimum** | Minimum 44×44pt interactive area (use hitSlop if icon is smaller). | Small icons without expanded tap area. | Meets accessibility and platform usability standards. |
300: | **Icon Alignment** | Align icons to text baseline and maintain consistent padding. | Misaligned icons or inconsistent spacing around them. | Prevents subtle visual imbalance that reduces perceived quality. |
301: | **Icon Contrast** | Follow WCAG contrast standards: 4.5:1 for small elements, 3:1 minimum for larger UI glyphs. | Low-contrast icons that blend into the background. | Ensures accessibility in both light and dark modes. |
302: 
303: 
304: ### Interaction (App)
305: 
306: | Rule | Do | Don't |
307: |------|----|----- |
308: | **Tap feedback** | Provide clear pressed feedback (ripple/opacity/elevation) within 80-150ms | No visual response on tap |
309: | **Animation timing** | Keep micro-interactions around 150-300ms with platform-native easing | Instant transitions or slow animations (>500ms) |
310: | **Accessibility focus** | Ensure screen reader focus order matches visual order and labels are descriptive | Unlabeled controls or confusing focus traversal |
311: | **Disabled state clarity** | Use disabled semantics (`disabled`/native disabled props), reduced emphasis, and no tap action | Controls that look tappable but do nothing |
312: | **Touch target minimum** | Keep tap areas >=44x44pt (iOS) or >=48x48dp (Android), expand hit area when icon is smaller | Tiny tap targets or icon-only hit areas without padding |
313: | **Gesture conflict prevention** | Keep one primary gesture per region and avoid nested tap/drag conflicts | Overlapping gestures causing accidental actions |
314: | **Semantic native controls** | Prefer native interactive primitives (`Button`, `Pressable`, platform equivalents) with proper accessibility roles | Generic containers used as primary controls without semantics |
315: 
316: ### Light/Dark Mode Contrast
317: 
318: | Rule | Do | Don't |
319: |------|----|----- |
320: | **Surface readability (light)** | Keep cards/surfaces clearly separated from background with sufficient opacity/elevation | Overly transparent surfaces that blur hierarchy |
321: | **Text contrast (light)** | Maintain body text contrast >=4.5:1 against light surfaces | Low-contrast gray body text |
322: | **Text contrast (dark)** | Maintain primary text contrast >=4.5:1 and secondary text >=3:1 on dark surfaces | Dark mode text that blends into background |
323: | **Border and divider visibility** | Ensure separators are visible in both themes (not just light mode) | Theme-specific borders disappearing in one mode |
324: | **State contrast parity** | Keep pressed/focused/disabled states equally distinguishable in light and dark themes | Defining interaction states for one theme only |
325: | **Token-driven theming** | Use semantic color tokens mapped per theme across app surfaces/text/icons | Hardcoded per-screen hex values |
326: | **Scrim and modal legibility** | Use a modal scrim strong enough to isolate foreground content (typically 40-60% black) | Weak scrim that leaves background visually competing |
327: 
328: ### Layout & Spacing
329: 
330: | Rule | Do | Don't |
331: |------|----|----- |
332: | **Safe-area compliance** | Respect top/bottom safe areas for all fixed headers, tab bars, and CTA bars | Placing fixed UI under notch, status bar, or gesture area |
333: | **System bar clearance** | Add spacing for status/navigation bars and gesture home indicator | Let tappable content collide with OS chrome |
334: | **Consistent content width** | Keep predictable content width per device class (phone/tablet) | Mixing arbitrary widths between screens |
335: | **8dp spacing rhythm** | Use a consistent 4/8dp spacing system for padding/gaps/section spacing | Random spacing increments with no rhythm |
336: | **Readable text measure** | Keep long-form text readable on large devices (avoid edge-to-edge paragraphs on tablets) | Full-width long text that hurts readability |
337: | **Section spacing hierarchy** | Define clear vertical rhythm tiers (e.g., 16/24/32/48) by hierarchy | Similar UI levels with inconsistent spacing |
338: | **Adaptive gutters by breakpoint** | Increase horizontal insets on larger widths and in landscape | Same narrow gutter on all device sizes/orientations |
339: | **Scroll and fixed element coexistence** | Add bottom/top content insets so lists are not hidden behind fixed bars | Scroll content obscured by sticky headers/footers |
340: 
341: ---
342: 
343: ## Pre-Delivery Checklist
344: 
345: Before delivering UI code, verify these items:
346: Scope notice: This checklist is for App UI (iOS/Android/React Native/Flutter).
347: 
348: ### Visual Quality
349: - [ ] No emojis used as icons (use SVG instead)
350: - [ ] All icons come from a consistent icon family and style
351: - [ ] Official brand assets are used with correct proportions and clear space
352: - [ ] Pressed-state visuals do not shift layout bounds or cause jitter
353: - [ ] Semantic theme tokens are used consistently (no ad-hoc per-screen hardcoded colors)
354: 
355: ### Interaction
356: - [ ] All tappable elements provide clear pressed feedback (ripple/opacity/elevation)
357: - [ ] Touch targets meet minimum size (>=44x44pt iOS, >=48x48dp Android)
358: - [ ] Micro-interaction timing stays in the 150-300ms range with native-feeling easing
359: - [ ] Disabled states are visually clear and non-interactive
360: - [ ] Screen reader focus order matches visual order, and interactive labels are descriptive
361: - [ ] Gesture regions avoid nested/conflicting interactions (tap/drag/back-swipe conflicts)
362: 
363: ### Light/Dark Mode
364: - [ ] Primary text contrast >=4.5:1 in both light and dark mode
365: - [ ] Secondary text contrast >=3:1 in both light and dark mode
366: - [ ] Dividers/borders and interaction states are distinguishable in both modes
367: - [ ] Modal/drawer scrim opacity is strong enough to preserve foreground legibility (typically 40-60% black)
368: - [ ] Both themes are tested before delivery (not inferred from a single theme)
369: 
370: ### Layout
371: - [ ] Safe areas are respected for headers, tab bars, and bottom CTA bars
372: - [ ] Scroll content is not hidden behind fixed/sticky bars
373: - [ ] Verified on small phone, large phone, and tablet (portrait + landscape)
374: - [ ] Horizontal insets/gutters adapt correctly by device size and orientation
375: - [ ] 4/8dp spacing rhythm is maintained across component, section, and page levels
376: - [ ] Long-form text measure remains readable on larger devices (no edge-to-edge paragraphs)
377: 
378: ### Accessibility
379: - [ ] All meaningful images/icons have accessibility labels
380: - [ ] Form fields have labels, hints, and clear error messages
381: - [ ] Color is not the only indicator
382: - [ ] Reduced motion and dynamic text size are supported without layout breakage
383: - [ ] Accessibility traits/roles/states (selected, disabled, expanded) are announced correctly

941358249.content:
--- /home/johnwick/Project/belajar-nextjs/.gemini/skills/design/SKILL.md (Lines 1-313 of 313) ---
1: ---
2: name: design
3: description: "Comprehensive design skill: brand identity, design tokens, UI styling, logo generation (55 styles, Gemini AI), corporate identity program (50 deliverables, CIP mockups), HTML presentations (Chart.js), banner design (22 styles, social/ads/web/print), icon design (15 styles, SVG, Gemini 3.1 Pro), social photos (HTML→screenshot, multi-platform). Actions: design logo, create CIP, generate mockups, build slides, design banner, generate icon, create social photos, social media images, brand identity, design system. Platforms: Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, YouTube, Instagram, Pinterest, TikTok, Threads, Google Ads."
4: argument-hint: "[design-type] [context]"
5: license: MIT
6: metadata:
7:   author: claudekit
8:   version: "2.1.0"
9: ---
10: 
11: # Design
12: 
13: Unified design skill: brand, tokens, UI, logo, CIP, slides, banners, social photos, icons.
14: 
15: ## When to Use
16: 
17: - Brand identity, voice, assets
18: - Design system tokens and specs
19: - UI styling with shadcn/ui + Tailwind
20: - Logo design and AI generation
21: - Corporate identity program (CIP) deliverables
22: - Presentations and pitch decks
23: - Banner design for social media, ads, web, print
24: - Social photos for Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter, Pinterest, TikTok
25: 
26: ## Sub-skill Routing
27: 
28: | Task | Sub-skill | Details |
29: |------|-----------|---------|
30: | Brand identity, voice, assets | `brand` | External skill |
31: | Tokens, specs, CSS vars | `design-system` | External skill |
32: | shadcn/ui, Tailwind, code | `ui-styling` | External skill |
33: | Logo creation, AI generation | Logo (built-in) | `references/logo-design.md` |
34: | CIP mockups, deliverables | CIP (built-in) | `references/cip-design.md` |
35: | Presentations, pitch decks | Slides (built-in) | `references/slides.md` |
36: | Banners, covers, headers | Banner (built-in) | `references/banner-sizes-and-styles.md` |
37: | Social media images/photos | Social Photos (built-in) | `references/social-photos-design.md` |
38: | SVG icons, icon sets | Icon (built-in) | `references/icon-design.md` |
39: 
40: ## Logo Design (Built-in)
41: 
42: 55+ styles, 30 color palettes, 25 industry guides. Gemini Nano Banana models.
43: 
44: ### Logo: Generate Design Brief
45: 
46: ```bash
47: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/logo/search.py "tech startup modern" --design-brief -p "BrandName"
48: ```
49: 
50: ### Logo: Search Styles/Colors/Industries
51: 
52: ```bash
53: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/logo/search.py "minimalist clean" --domain style
54: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/logo/search.py "tech professional" --domain color
55: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/logo/search.py "healthcare medical" --domain industry
56: ```
57: 
58: ### Logo: Generate with AI
59: 
60: **ALWAYS** generate output logo images with white background.
61: 
62: ```bash
63: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/logo/generate.py --brand "TechFlow" --style minimalist --industry tech
64: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/logo/generate.py --prompt "coffee shop vintage badge" --style vintage
65: ```
66: 
67: **IMPORTANT:** When scripts fail, try to fix them directly.
68: 
69: After generation, **ALWAYS** ask user about HTML preview via `AskUserQuestion`. If yes, invoke `/ui-ux-pro-max` for gallery.
70: 
71: ## CIP Design (Built-in)
72: 
73: 50+ deliverables, 20 styles, 20 industries. Gemini Nano Banana (Flash/Pro).
74: 
75: ### CIP: Generate Brief
76: 
77: ```bash
78: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/search.py "tech startup" --cip-brief -b "BrandName"
79: ```
80: 
81: ### CIP: Search Domains
82: 
83: ```bash
84: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/search.py "business card letterhead" --domain deliverable
85: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/search.py "luxury premium elegant" --domain style
86: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/search.py "hospitality hotel" --domain industry
87: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/search.py "office reception" --domain mockup
88: ```
89: 
90: ### CIP: Generate Mockups
91: 
92: ```bash
93: # With logo (RECOMMENDED)
94: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/generate.py --brand "TopGroup" --logo /path/to/logo.png --deliverable "business card" --industry "consulting"
95: 
96: # Full CIP set
97: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/generate.py --brand "TopGroup" --logo /path/to/logo.png --industry "consulting" --set
98: 
99: # Pro model (4K text)
100: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/generate.py --brand "TopGroup" --logo logo.png --deliverable "business card" --model pro
101: 
102: # Without logo
103: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/generate.py --brand "TechFlow" --deliverable "business card" --no-logo-prompt
104: ```
105: 
106: Models: `flash` (default, `gemini-2.5-flash-image`), `pro` (`gemini-3-pro-image-preview`)
107: 
108: ### CIP: Render HTML Presentation
109: 
110: ```bash
111: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/cip/render-html.py --brand "TopGroup" --industry "consulting" --images /path/to/cip-output
112: ```
113: 
114: **Tip:** If no logo exists, use Logo Design section above first.
115: 
116: ## Slides (Built-in)
117: 
118: Strategic HTML presentations with Chart.js, design tokens, copywriting formulas.
119: 
120: Load `references/slides-create.md` for the creation workflow.
121: 
122: ### Slides: Knowledge Base
123: 
124: | Topic | File |
125: |-------|------|
126: | Creation Guide | `references/slides-create.md` |
127: | Layout Patterns | `references/slides-layout-patterns.md` |
128: | HTML Template | `references/slides-html-template.md` |
129: | Copywriting | `references/slides-copywriting-formulas.md` |
130: | Strategies | `references/slides-strategies.md` |
131: 
132: ## Banner Design (Built-in)
133: 
134: 22 art direction styles across social, ads, web, print. Uses `frontend-design`, `ai-artist`, `ai-multimodal`, `chrome-devtools` skills.
135: 
136: Load `references/banner-sizes-and-styles.md` for complete sizes and styles reference.
137: 
138: ### Banner: Workflow
139: 
140: 1. **Gather requirements** via `AskUserQuestion` — purpose, platform, content, brand, style, quantity
141: 2. **Research** — Activate `ui-ux-pro-max`, browse Pinterest for references
142: 3. **Design** — Create HTML/CSS banner with `frontend-design`, generate visuals with `ai-artist`/`ai-multimodal`
143: 4. **Export** — Screenshot to PNG at exact dimensions via `chrome-devtools`
144: 5. **Present** — Show all options side-by-side, iterate on feedback
145: 
146: ### Banner: Quick Size Reference
147: 
148: | Platform | Type | Size (px) |
149: |----------|------|-----------|
150: | Facebook | Cover | 820 x 312 |
151: | Twitter/X | Header | 1500 x 500 |
152: | LinkedIn | Personal | 1584 x 396 |
153: | YouTube | Channel art | 2560 x 1440 |
154: | Instagram | Story | 1080 x 1920 |
155: | Instagram | Post | 1080 x 1080 |
156: | Google Ads | Med Rectangle | 300 x 250 |
157: | Website | Hero | 1920 x 600-1080 |
158: 
159: ### Banner: Top Art Styles
160: 
161: | Style | Best For |
162: |-------|----------|
163: | Minimalist | SaaS, tech |
164: | Bold Typography | Announcements |
165: | Gradient | Modern brands |
166: | Photo-Based | Lifestyle, e-com |
167: | Geometric | Tech, fintech |
168: | Glassmorphism | SaaS, apps |
169: | Neon/Cyberpunk | Gaming, events |
170: 
171: ### Banner: Design Rules
172: 
173: - Safe zones: critical content in central 70-80%
174: - One CTA per banner, bottom-right, min 44px height
175: - Max 2 fonts, min 16px body, ≥32px headline
176: - Text under 20% for ads (Meta penalizes)
177: - Print: 300 DPI, CMYK, 3-5mm bleed
178: 
179: ## Icon Design (Built-in)
180: 
181: 15 styles, 12 categories. Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview generates SVG text output.
182: 
183: ### Icon: Generate Single Icon
184: 
185: ```bash
186: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/icon/generate.py --prompt "settings gear" --style outlined
187: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/icon/generate.py --prompt "shopping cart" --style filled --color "#6366F1"
188: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/icon/generate.py --name "dashboard" --category navigation --style duotone
189: ```
190: 
191: ### Icon: Generate Batch Variations
192: 
193: ```bash
194: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/icon/generate.py --prompt "cloud upload" --batch 4 --output-dir ./icons
195: ```
196: 
197: ### Icon: Multi-size Export
198: 
199: ```bash
200: python3 ~/.claude/skills/design/scripts/icon/generate.py --prompt "user profile" --sizes "16,24,32,48" --output-dir ./icons
201: ```
202: 
203: ### Icon: Top Styles
204: 
205: | Style | Best For |
206: |-------|----------|
207: | outlined | UI interfaces, web apps |
208: | filled | Mobile apps, nav bars |
209: | duotone | Marketing, landing pages |
210: | rounded | Friendly apps, health |
211: | sharp | Tech, fintech, enterprise |
212: | flat | Material design, Google-style |
213: | gradient | Modern brands, SaaS |
214: 
215: **Model:** `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` — text-only output (SVG is XML text). No image generation API needed.
216: 
217: ## Social Photos (Built-in)
218: 
219: Multi-platform social image design: HTML/CSS → screenshot export. Uses `ui-ux-pro-max`, `brand`, `design-system`, `chrome-devtools` skills.
220: 
221: Load `references/social-photos-design.md` for sizes, templates, best practices.
222: 
223: ### Social Photos: Workflow
224: 
225: 1. **Orchestrate** — `project-management` skill for TODO tasks; parallel subagents for independent work
226: 2. **Analyze** — Parse prompt: subject, platforms, style, brand context, content elements
227: 3. **Ideate** — 3-5 concepts, present via `AskUserQuestion`
228: 4. **Design** — `/ckm:brand` → `/ckm:design-system` → randomly invoke `/ck:ui-ux-pro-max` OR `/ck:frontend-design`; HTML per idea × size
229: 5. **Export** — `chrome-devtools` or Playwright screenshot at exact px (2x deviceScaleFactor)
230: 6. **Verify** — Use Chrome MCP or `chrome-devtools` skill to visually inspect exported designs; fix layout/styling issues and re-export
231: 7. **Report** — Summary to `plans/reports/` with design decisions
232: 8. **Organize** — Invoke `assets-organizing` skill to sort output files and reports
233: 
234: ### Social Photos: Key Sizes
235: 
236: | Platform | Size (px) | Platform | Size (px) |
237: |----------|-----------|----------|-----------|
238: | IG Post | 1080×1080 | FB Post | 1200×630 |
239: | IG Story | 1080×1920 | X Post | 1200×675 |
240: | IG Carousel | 1080×1350 | LinkedIn | 1200×627 |
241: | YT Thumb | 1280×720 | Pinterest | 1000×1500 |
242: 
243: ## Workflows
244: 
245: ### Complete Brand Package
246: 
247: 1. **Logo** → `scripts/logo/generate.py` → Generate logo variants
248: 2. **CIP** → `scripts/cip/generate.py --logo ...` → Create deliverable mockups
249: 3. **Presentation** → Load `references/slides-create.md` → Build pitch deck
250: 
251: ### New Design System
252: 
253: 1. **Brand** (brand skill) → Define colors, typography, voice
254: 2. **Tokens** (design-system skill) → Create semantic token layers
255: 3. **Implement** (ui-styling skill) → Configure Tailwind, shadcn/ui
256: 
257: ## References
258: 
259: | Topic | File |
260: |-------|------|
261: | Design Routing | `references/design-routing.md` |
262: | Logo Design Guide | `references/logo-design.md` |
263: | Logo Styles | `references/logo-style-guide.md` |
264: | Logo Colors | `references/logo-color-psychology.md` |
265: | Logo Prompts | `references/logo-prompt-engineering.md` |
266: | CIP Design Guide | `references/cip-design.md` |
267: | CIP Deliverables | `references/cip-deliverable-guide.md` |
268: | CIP Styles | `references/cip-style-guide.md` |
269: | CIP Prompts | `references/cip-prompt-engineering.md` |
270: | Slides Create | `references/slides-create.md` |
271: | Slides Layouts | `references/slides-layout-patterns.md` |
272: | Slides Template | `references/slides-html-template.md` |
273: | Slides Copy | `references/slides-copywriting-formulas.md` |
274: | Slides Strategy | `references/slides-strategies.md` |
275: | Banner Sizes & Styles | `references/banner-sizes-and-styles.md` |
276: | Social Photos Guide | `references/social-photos-design.md` |
277: | Icon Design Guide | `references/icon-design.md` |
278: 
279: ## Scripts
280: 
281: | Script | Purpose |
282: |--------|---------|
283: | `scripts/logo/search.py` | Search logo styles, colors, industries |
284: | `scripts/logo/generate.py` | Generate logos with Gemini AI |
285: | `scripts/logo/core.py` | BM25 search engine for logo data |
286: | `scripts/cip/search.py` | Search CIP deliverables, styles, industries |
287: | `scripts/cip/generate.py` | Generate CIP mockups with Gemini |
288: | `scripts/cip/render-html.py` | Render HTML presentation from CIP mockups |
289: | `scripts/cip/core.py` | BM25 search engine for CIP data |
290: | `scripts/icon/generate.py` | Generate SVG icons with Gemini 3.1 Pro |
291: 
292: ## Prerequisites
293: 
294: **Python:** This skill uses Python scripts. On Windows, use `python` instead of `python3` (e.g., `python scripts/logo/search.py` instead of `python3 scripts/logo/search.py`).
295: 
296: Check if Python is installed:
297: ```bash
298: python3 --version || python --version
299: ```
300: 
301: ## Setup
302: 
303: ```bash
304: export GEMINI_API_KEY="your-key"  # https://aistudio.google.com/apikey
305: pip install google-genai pillow
306: ```
307: 
308: > **Note for Windows:** Use `python` instead of `pip` where needed (e.g., `python -m pip install ...`).
309: 
310: ## Integration
311: 
312: **External sub-skills:** brand, design-system, ui-styling
313: **Related Skills:** frontend-design, ui-ux-pro-max, ai-multimodal, chrome-devtools
941358249.name: ReadFile
